content.claim: Poland's reported special educational needs identification for autism increased 30% in a single year (2022/2023 school year to 82,199 children), with regional increases exceeding 1,000% in some provinces, and the male-to-female diagnostic ratio declined from 4.56 (2012) to 2.99 (2023), suggesting improved recognition of autism in females
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: The causal attribution to real prevalence increase versus improved diagnostics versus finance incentive effects is explicitly contested in the source itself
Baseline year 2012 figures (18,924) and 2022/23 figures (82,199) may not use identical diagnostic categories, preventing direct prevalence comparison
Regional variation data does not explain underlying drivers (assessment capacity, awareness, funding changes, diagnostic criteria shifts)
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
